definitions: A novel by Jane Austen, first published in 1813, which chronicles the emotional development of Elizabeth Bennet, who learns the error of making hasty judgments and comes to appreciate the difference between superficial and essential goodness.
A romantic drama film based on Jane Austen's 1813 novel of the same name, directed by Joe Wright and released in 2005.
A 1940 American film based on the novel by Jane Austen, starring Laurence Olivier and Greer Garson.
examples: Pride and Prejudice is one of the most beloved novels in English literature.
Many adaptations of Pride and Prejudice have been made for film and television.
I recently re-read Pride and Prejudice and enjoyed it even more the second time.
